hook.text: The Yangtze is Asia's longest river and the third longest on Earth at 6,300 km.

buildup.text: It divides China into north and south, shaping culture, dialect, and cuisine.

discovery.text: The Three Gorges Dam, the world's largest power station, harnesses its flow.

twist.text: Building the dam displaced 1.3 million people and drowned ancient towns.

climax.text: The Yangtze river dolphin went extinct in 2006—the first dolphin lost to humans.

punchline.text: One dam powers millions and silenced a species.

quiz.question: What happened to the Yangtze river dolphin?
options: It was relocated to the Amazon
